architecture.summary: Representation Engineering (RepE) is a top-down transparency framework that treats population-level representations (rather than individual neurons or circuits) as the primary unit of analysis in deep neural networks. The system comprises two main components: (1) Representation Reading, which uses Linear Artificial Tomography (LAT)—a three-step process of stimulus design, neural activity collection at specific token positions, and PCA-based linear model fitting—to extract directions in representation space that correlate with high-level concepts (truthfulness, utility, emotion) and functions (honesty, power-seeking); (2) Representation Control, which modifies these representations via reading vectors, contrast vectors (stimulus-dependent difference vectors), or low-rank adapters (LoRRA) using linear combination, piecewise, or projection operations to steer model behavior. Information flows from input stimuli through frozen model layers, where representations are read or transformed, then back through the model to influence outputs.
trained_components: Low-rank adapter matrices in LoRRA baseline
Linear model (PCA direction) in LAT reading baseline

biblio.title: Representation Engineering: A Top-Down Approach to AI Transparency

claims.statement: LAT outperforms zero-shot heuristic scoring on TruthfulQA MC1, achieving state-of-the-art results and improving over standard evaluation by multiple percentage points.
LAT outperforms few-shot prompting on traditional QA benchmarks (OpenbookQA, CommonSenseQA, RACE, ARC) by a notable margin.
LAT outperforms prior methods such as CCS (Burns et al., 2022) by a wide margin on DeBERTa evaluated on common benchmarks.

Representation reading via LAT enables detection of imitative falsehoods and intentional lies: models possess a consistent internal concept of truthfulness that can be extracted and used to classify statement veracity at higher accuracy than output-level scoring.

RepE techniques provide traction on a wide range of safety-relevant problems including honesty, harmlessness, power-seeking, utility estimation, knowledge editing, jailbreaking, memorization, emotional state tracking, and fairness.
Contrast vectors (stimulus-dependent difference vectors computed at inference time) prove significantly stronger control baselines than stimulus-independent reading vectors.
LAT with unlabeled or self-generated stimuli is effective for extracting representations of concepts, avoiding annotation bias and enabling extraction of superhuman representations.

Representation engineering follows a Hopfieldian (rather than Sherringtonian) view of cognition: cognition is a product of representational spaces and population-level activity patterns rather than node-to-node connections, enabling top-down transparency research at the level of emergent high-level cognitive phenomena.
